def: (Pers. ميغ Sans. मेघ ) s. m. Rain. مينـﮩ برسنا meṅh barasnā, To rain. مينـٿـﮩ چهوٿنا meṅh chhūṭnā, To rain hard.

n. m. S. मेघ Pr. meho ; A. mā water. Rain; a shower. Be-meṅh ke daṅvrī, ghoṛā binā lagām, Be-māth ke laskar, tīnoṅ bhail na-kām . Bhoj. Prov. Plough the soil without rain, have a horse with no rein, Or a force with no lead, three things no good can breed. meṅh ānā , v. n. To threaten (rain). meṅh barasnā, yā paṛnā , v. n. To rain. 1. Meṅh barastā hai jab ṭaṭīrī pānī pītī hai . 2. Meṅh to nahīṅ baras rahā hai? It's not raining, is it? (What's your hurry?)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑁂𑀳 (meha), from Sanskrit मेघ (megha, “cloud”), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hmaygʰás (“cloud”). Doublet of میغ (meġ).

def: s.f. = مينہدي meṅhdī, q.v.

ety: for meṅdhī = S. मेन्धिका
def: s.f. The Henna plant, or Broad Egyptian Privet, Lawsonia alba, or L. inermis, or L. spinosa (cultivated throughout India for its leaves, and as a hedge-plant for gardens; the powdered leaves, beaten up with catechu and made into paste are much used by women to dye their hands and feet a reddish-orange; and, by men, to dye their beards; and occasionally to stain the tails and manes of horses); — the marriage-feast on the occasion of the bride's hands and feet being stained with henna (ḥinā); — an ark, or tabernacle, carried in solemn procession by Muḥammadans on the eve of the anniversary of the death of a person who died just as he was about to marry
subs: (-meṅ), To apply henna (to, — as a cure for a blister, &c.)
or meṇhdī lagānā (-meṅ), To stain (the hands and feet, &c.) with henna; to apply henna (to).

def: n. f. S. मेन्धी 1. Myrtle (حنا). Tumhāre kyā hāthoṅ meṅ meṅhdī lag rahī hai? 2. A marriage ceremony (Mah.). 3. An ark or tabernacle. It is carried in solemn procession by Musalmans, more especially on the eve of the anniversary of the death of a saint who happens to die when he is about to marry. meṅhdī bāṅdhnā , v. a. To apply henna (a cure for a blister). meṅhdī lagānā, yā rachānā , v. a. To stain the palms, etc., with meṅhdī .
more: s. f. The name of a plant from the leaves of which a red die is prepared, with which the natives stain their hands and feet. (Lawsonia inermis).

def: (n. of instr. fr. عرج 'to ascend'), s.f. & m. more: n. f. عرج Ascension (of Mahommed to heaven).
(noun of instrument of عرج Heb. ) s. m. f. 1. A ladder, stair, any thing by which one ascends. 2. Ascent, ascension.

(˚yā+āć˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Behaving falsely; — a deceiver, &c.
s.f. False doctrine, heresy, denial of future existence, atheism
(˚yā+ar˚), adv. For the sake of falsehood, with a view to deception
s.m. (f. -kartrī), One who takes a false oath, a perjuror
s.f. Erroneous opinion, error, mistake; ignorance
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Speaking untruly, lying; denying a charge; — a liar.

ety: mīćh˚, mīć˚ = Prk. मिच्छ(इ), or मेच्छ(इ) = S. मेक्ष्य(ति), future (used in sense of present) of rt. मिष्
def: v.t. To shut or close (the eyes); to wink (cf. mićnā).
more: v. a. To close the eyes, to shut, to wink.

ety: mićkār˚ = S. मृक्ष (or मृज) + कार
def: v.t. To rinse.

ety: mīćh˚, mīć˚ = Prk. मिच्छ(इ), or मेच्छ(इ) = S. मेक्ष्य(ति), future (used in sense of present) of rt. मिष्
def: v.t. To shut or close (the eyes); to wink (cf. mićnā).
more: v. a. To close the eyes, to shut, to wink.

ety: fr. the trans. mīćnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be shut or closed (the eye); to shut, close; — v.t. = mīćnā, q.v.

ety: mićrā˚ = mićār˚ = S. मृक्ष+कार (or मृश + कार), cf. mić-kārnā, or ˚rā = ˚ār = āl, is the H. caus. augment
def: v.t. To play with food (by turning it over), to eat without appetite.
more: v. a. To eat without an appetite.

def: s.m. = midḥat, q.v. s.f. = madḥ, q.v. s.f. Praising; praise, eulogium, encomium, commendation; applause; approbation; recommendation
subs: s.m. Eulogist, encomiast, panegyrist; a bard

ety: for A. مدحة, inf. n. of مدح
def: s.f. = madḥ, q.v. s.f. Praising; praise, eulogium, encomium, commendation; applause; approbation; recommendation
subs: s.m. Eulogist, encomiast, panegyrist; a bard

ety: n. of instr. fr. فتح 'to open'
def: s.f. An instrument with which a lock is opened; a key.
more: (noun of instrum. from فتح Heb. and Aram. ) s. A key.

ety: n. of instr. fr. غفر 'to cover,' &c.
def: s.m. Mail, or network of steel, worn under the helmet, or cap, or hat; — a helmet.
more: (noun of instrum. from غفر) s. m. A helmet.

ety: Gr. μαγνῆτις
def: s.m. The magnet, loadstone (also written mag̠nat̤īs, or mag̠nīt̤īs, and mig̠nāt̤īs).
more: (or مغنطيس or مغنيطيس Μαγνῆτις ) s. m. The magnet or loadstone. See مقناطيس miḳnāt̤īs.

def: (n. of inst. fr. غال (for غول) 'to destroy'), s.m. lit. 'An instrument with which a thing is destroyed'; a knife, or a piece of iron, inserted in a whip or stick (which is as a sheath to it); — a long slender sword with a flat back; — a long spear-head with the end bent back; — a proper name (of a man).
more: s. 1. A piece of iron enclosed in a whip or stick, and the likeness of a short sword but thinner and longer, a long spear head or slender sword with the end bent back. 2. Name of a man.

ety: Zend maś, or maz; S. महा
def: adj. Great; chief, principal.
more: adj. Great. ¶ mah (contract. of ماه Sans. माः ) s. m. 1. The moon. مه پاره mah-pāra, A beauty, a mistress. 2. A month. See under ماه māh.

ety: fr. مہد 'to spread equally; to make (a bed
def: s.m. A bed; sofa; chair; throne; any place on which one sits or reclines.
(from مـﮩد) s. m. A throne, sofa, bed, chair.

ety: for A. محفة miḥaffat
def: s.m. See محافه muḥāfa.

ety: instrum. n. fr. حكّ 'to scratch, to rub,' &c.
def: s.f. The stone upon which pieces of money, &c. are rubbed to try their quality; touchstone; test.
s.f. pl. (of محنت měḥnat), Labours; troubles, afflictions, &c.
more: s. plur. (of محنت) Labours, troubles, afflictions.

ety: mihā˚ = S. मेघ;; Prk. मेह+ā = āw = Prk. āwa, or āwe = S. आपि (caus. aug.); or = S. मिह्+ā, &c.
def: v.n. To become damp or humid.
s. f. A churn.
s. f. Name of a plant. See مينـﮩدي meṅhdī.
more: s. m. Leader, guide; the name of the twelfth Imām, in the creed of the Shīʼaﻌs, who is still believed by them to be living.

ety: mih, q.v.+īn = Zend yaṅh = S. इयस् or यस्, nom. इयान् or यान्
def: adj. Fine, subtle; thin (not coarse); — greater, greatest; elder-born
subs: (or mahīn) āwāz, s.f. A fine or shrill sound; a high note; the treble; — a soprano voice; — a thin, or feeble, voice
s.m. A tailor; one who performs any fine needle-work

def: s.m. The sun; the moon; a cloud; wind, air (cf. next).
wety: Ultimately from Sanskrit महाराज (mahārāja) ?

def: s. f. Entertainment, hospitality, a feast, banquet. مـﮩماني کرنا mihmānī karnā, To entertain.
s. f. See مينـﮩدي meṅhdī.

ety: Pārsī mihir; old P. mithra; Pehl. mitn; Zend mithra; S. मित्र
def: s.f. Love, affection, friendship, kindness, favour; mercy, pity, sympathy, feeling; — prosperity
wety: Ultimately from Sanskrit महाराज (mahārāja) ?

ety: = S. मिहिर
def: s.m. The sun; — name of a Persian month in which falls the autumnal equinox (the month of September)
wety: Ultimately from Sanskrit महाराज (mahārāja) ?

def: adj. Friendly, kind, affectionate, indulgent, gracious; (subst.) A friend.

ety: mihr+bān, qq.v.
def: adj. Loving, affectionate, friendly, kind, benevolent, beneficent, favouring, indulgent, gracious, propitious; compassionate, merciful; — s.m. A friend; — mihrbānā, O friend! Dear Sir! — mihr-bān honā (-par), To be kind, or gracious, or favourable (to); to favour (with, as a visit, &c.); to feel pity (for), to take compassion (on).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مِهْرَبَان (mihrabān).

def: s.f. (colloq.), corr. of next, q.v.
s. f. See مـﮩرباني mihrbān-ī.

ety: mihrbān, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Friendliness, kindness, goodness, favour
subs: To show kindness (to), to treat kindly
(-par), To show friendship or kindness (to), to be kind or gracious (to), to confer favour (on), to favour, to befriend
It is your kindness or favour; it is very good or kind of you; thank you.

ety: fr. mihr
def: s.f. Affection, friendship, kindness (i.q. mihr; but only used as last member of compounds); — a kind of lute; — a psaltery, a sackbut.
s. f. A woman.
s. f. A woman.
adj. (dakh.) Affectionate, friendly, kind.
s.f. See s.v. mihr. s.m. The sun; — name of a Persian month in which falls the autumnal equinox (the month of September)
(from مـﮩر mihr, Friendship, and ورز warz, part. act. in compos. of ورزيدن To sow, practise, exercise, study) s. f. Exercise of friendship, kindness.
adj. Like the sun.
s. f. Chiefship.
s. f. Female of mihtar, sign. 3. q. v. Also, a female innkeeper.
s. m. (dakh.) Rain: see مينـﮩ meṅh, and مهيون mhyūṅ.
s.m. corr. of مزاج mizāj, q.v. adj. Proud, haughty, conceited
subs: adj. & s.m. Knowing (or one who knows) the temperament or constitution; — a confidant
(or mizāj-sharīf), or mizāj-ě-ʻālī, or mizāj-ě-mubārak, interrog. Your noble, or exalted, or blessed disposition or health (how is it?), how is your Honour's health? how do you do?
To show temper; — to give oneself airs
(-kā), To discover or understand the disposition or temper or humour (of); to understand or fathom (a person)
v.n. To fit in with the humour, to suit the temper or humour (of, -ke), to please; to like; — to stand upon (one's) dignity, to be proud
adj. Of a pleasant disposition; good-humoured

ety: n. of place fr. جمر 'to give live coals to'
def: s.m. A vessel for fumigation; a censer; a chafing-dish; — incense; perfume.
more: (noun of place from جمر Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. 1. A chafingdish. 2. A censer for incense. 3. Perfume.

def: v.t. = مينجنا mīṅjnā, q.v. v.t. To rub with the hands; to crush, pulverize; to rub, scrub, scour, clean (i.q. māṅjnā, q.v.).
more: v. a. To rub with the hands.

ety: P. mījū, or mīzhū
def: s.m. Lentil (i.q. masūr, q.v.); pease, pulse.
prop. n. The archangel Michael.
wety: Learned borrowing from Arabic مِيكَائِيل (mīkāʔīl), ultimately from Biblical Hebrew מִיכָאֵל (miká'el).

def: n. prop. The archangel Michael.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قطّ 'to cut'
def: s.m. The thing (generally a piece of bone or ivory) upon which the reed for writing is nibbed.
more: (noun of instrum. of قطّ Chald. ) s. m. A piece of bone or bamboo on which they nib their writing-reeds or pens.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قدر 'to measure,' &c.
def: s.m. Measure; quantity, quantum; magnitude, size, bulk, dimension; proportion, extent; space; amount, sum, number attained; — rate; settled allowance; — term (of life)
more: n. m. قدر 1. Magnitude; size; dimension. 2. Quantity; weight. 3. Amount (جمع 1). 4. Rate; settled allowance. 5. Measure (اندازه 3). 6. ( Math .) A quantity. miqdār-i-majhūl , ( Alg .) An unknown quantity. miqdār-i-mārūf , ( Alg .) A known quantity. miqdār-i-muqarrarah . A fixed amount or quantity.
(from قدر Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Quantity, magnitude, measure, size, dimension, space, number.
wety: From Arabic مِقْدَار (miqdār).

ety: n. of instr. fr. کحل 'to anoint the eyes with kǒhl or "antimony"'
def: s.m. The instrument with which collyrium is applied to the eyes.
more: (noun of instrum. of کحل) s. m. The instrument with which collyrium is applied to the eyes.

def: v.t. To beat, subdue, overcome.
more: v. a. To beat, to subdue, to overcome.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قنع 'to turn downwards,' &c.
def: s.m. A veil of muslin or fine linen (esp. a bride's veil) worn over the head and reaching almost to the ground.
more: n. m. قنع A bridal veil worn over the head and reaching almost to the ground.
prop. miḳnaﻌ (noun of instr. from قنع) s. m. A veil of muslin or other cloth worn over the head and reaching almost to the ground. (Chiefly, applied to a brideʼs veil).

ety: for A. مقنعة miqnaʻat, fem. of miqnaʻ
def: s.m. A veil of muslin or fine linen (esp. a bride's veil) worn over the head and reaching almost to the ground.

ety: for A. مکنة, v.n. fr. مکن 'to occupy rank or station,' &c.
def: s.f. Authority, power, ability, might, strength, force.

ety: fr. Gr. μαγνῆτις
def: s.m. The magnet, loadstone (also written mig̠nāt̤īs, q.v.; — syn. ćumbak).
more: s. m. The magnet, the loadstone. See مغناطيس mig̠ẖnāt̤īs.
wety: From Arabic مَغْنَاطِيس (maḡnāṭīs), ultimately of Ancient Greek origin. Akin to Pashto مقناطيس.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قرض 'to cut'
def: s.f. 'An instrument for shearing, or clipping, or cutting'; a pair of scissors; shears.
more: (noun of instrument of قرض Heb. ) s. f. Scissors, shears.

ety: n. of instr. fr. قطع 'to cut'
def: s.m. An instrument for cutting; shears (for cutting cloth, or silver, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Persian مَقْطَع (maqta').

ety: n. of instr. fr. قاس (for قيس
def: s.m. A measuring instrument, a measure; — a probe with which the depth of a wound is measured; — style (of a dial), gnomon; hand (of a watch); — the nilometer (of Egypt)
more: n. m. The stile of a dial; the hand of a watch; the nilometer in Egypt. miqyās-ul-harārat ; H. garmī-nāp , A thermometer. miqyās-ul-mā ; H. pānī-nāp , A hydrometer. miqyās-ul-mausim ; H. pavan-nāp , A barometer.
(noun of instrum. of قوس) s. m. 1. The style of a dial, gnomon, the hand of a watch, &c. 2. The Nilometer in Egypt.

ety: A. mīl
def: s.f. Probe (of a surgeon); a needle, bodkin, skewer, or wire (for anointing the eyes with collyrium; or, when heated, for blinding a person, by drawing it over the eyeballs); — barrel (of a gun).
more: (lit. one), n. m. See اتفاق 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, joṛ 6, 7. and milāo (1—4). 1. Mixture. Resham aur sūt kā mel . 2. Combination. 3. ( mel jol, mel milāp ) Association. Ghoṛe aur gadhe ke mel se khachchar hotā hai . 4. Connexion; relationship. 5. A mity. 6. Kind; sort. Yeh mel hamāre pās nahiṅ . Shopkeepers. mel-kā, melī , adj. 1. Of the same class or society. 2. Congenial. Yeh hamāre mel kā nahīṅ hai. mel k. , v. a. See milāo k. , and ittifāq k . 1. To associate with. 2. To watch. mel milāp yā mel jol h. , v. n. To be familiar with. be-mel , adj. See an-mel . 1. Not matched. 2. Unsuited. 3. Incoherent.
s. f. 1. A needle or bodkin, a skewer or wire instrument used to apply a collyrium to the eyes and when heated to blind people by drawing it hot before the eyes. 2. The barrel of a gun.

s. m. Connexion, relationship, agreement, combination, intimacy, concord, reconciliation, union, association, mixture, mixing; a meeting or assemblage of people, a set, a fair, a court of justice, a counterpart.

s. m. A mile. ¶ mail, s. m. 1. Inclination, tendency, bent, bias, wish, desire. 2. Affection, regard, partiality, fondness, attachment, aptitude. ميل رکهنا mail rakhnā, To associate with. ميل کرنا mail karnā, To close in with.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِيل (mīl), from Latin mīlle passuum.

ety: past. conj. part. of milnā, q.v.
def: part. = milkar or milke, q.v.
subs: adv. = mile-jule, q.v. part. Having united, having combined, &c.; — adv. In a body, in combination, in unity; together; unitedly; narmonionsly, on good terms
v.n. To go, or journey together; — to live in harmony
To dwell together in unity or harmony.

ety: A. also mīl, prob. fr. Lat. millia
def: s.m. A mile.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِيل (mīl), from Latin mīlle passuum.

ety: perf. part. of milnā, q.v.; — and = Prk. मिलिअओ; S. मिलित + कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Mixed; united, &c.; met with, found, obtained, &c. (see milnā)
subs: or milā-jhulā, adj. (f. -ī), United; in harmony, harmonious; sociable; friendly
adv. In union, united; in harmony, harmoniously, on good terms, amicably; sociably, &c.
s.m. pl. (Physics) A system of forces.

ety: dialec.
def: part. = mil-ke or mil-karke (see milnā).
s.f. pl. of millat, q.v.
more: s. plur. of ملت millat, q. v.

ety: S. मिलनं; — or i.q. milnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Coming into contact or union (with); contact; coalescence; union; mixing (with); agreement, harmony; meeting; encountering, &c. (see milnā).
more: s. m. Meeting, mixing, agreement, to meet, to mix, &c. See ملنا milnā.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मिलन (milana).

def: s.m. The act of closing the eyes; winking, blinking, twinkling.

ety: fr. milānā, q.v.
def: s.m. The bringing together or into contact; causing to mingle; forming a mixture; bringing about harmony, or reconciliation (between), adjustment (of differences, &c.), reconcilement, union; unison; producing harmony (in music); — comparing, comparison (of); — society, company; — host, army
more: n. f. 1. Comparison. 2. Adjustment. milān bojh, khās bojh , ( Phys .) Specific gravity. milān milānā , v. a. To adjust the accounts of land under cultivation. milān nazar , ( Ast .) Apparent size.
subs: s.m. Specific gravity; — milān milānā (-kā), To adjust the accounts (of land under cultivation, &c.)

ety: caus. of milnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To cause to unite or join, &c.; to unite, join, connect, attach; to close; to cement; — to cause to meet or come together; to bring together; to introduce (one person to another); to act as a pander or a pimp between; — to put (into, or amongst); to put (together, or with); to mix, mingle, blend, combine; to cause to unite, or agree, or correspond, or harmonize; to assimilate; to reconcile; to adjust; to apply; to fit; to accord; to compare (with, -se)
more: v. a. See ملنا v. n. 1. To bring together; join; introduce. 2. To act as a pimp. Kar fikr phand kīsau sau bāteṅ, sach meṅ jhūṭ milāte haiṅ, Khauf khatar zarā nahīṅ karte, dil meṅ mauj uṛāte haiṅ . Mauj. With truth they mix up lies, a hundred schemes they weave at will, No fear at all doth in them dwell, their hearts with pleasure thrill. milā lenā , v. a. To draw over.

(causal of ملنا Sans. मेलन ) v. a. 1. To mix, to blend. 2. To cause to meet, to join, attach, connect, adjust, to accord, to fit, unite, apply, introduce, to cement, close, compare, reconcile, assimilate, agree, find, get, meet, to visit. ¶ mulānā (from मूल्य ) v. a. To appraise.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To make friends of, to reconcile; to conciliate, to pacify; to persuade, to gain or win over, to make partizans of.
wety: Causative of ملنا (milnā).

ety: fr. milānā
def: s.f. Mixing, mingling; adjustment; comparison, &c. (see milāʼo).

ety: prob. Prk. मिलणिओ; S. मिलन+इकः
def: adj. Civil, affable, kind, &c. (i.q. milansār, q.v.); — faithful; worthy; — s.m. One who is sociable, or affable, &c.; — one who fraternizes (with others); an associate; a coadjutor.

ety: prob. Prk. मिलणिओ; S. मिलन+इकः
def: adj. Civil, affable, kind, &c. (i.q. milansār, q.v.); — faithful; worthy; — s.m. One who is sociable, or affable, &c.; — one who fraternizes (with others); an associate; a coadjutor.

ety: prob. Prk. मिलणिओ; S. मिलन+इकः
def: adj. Civil, affable, kind, &c. (i.q. milansār, q.v.); — faithful; worthy; — s.m. One who is sociable, or affable, &c.; — one who fraternizes (with others); an associate; a coadjutor.

ety: S. मिलन+सारिन्(?), rt. सृ
def: adj. Sociable, friendly, familiar, intimate; civil, affable; convivial.
more: adj. Civil, affable, sociable, friendly, familiar, intimate, convivial.
wety: From ملن (milan, “meeting, coming together”) + ـسار (-sār).

ety: milansār, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Sociableness, friendliness, affability, civility; geniality; concord, agreement, harmony.
more: n. f. Sociableness; geniality; friendliness. mil'nī , n. f. 1. ( milāvā ) A meeting; visit; reception. 2. A present made by the visitor, and esp. by the relations of the bride.
s. f. Sociableness, friendliness, affability, civility, concord, agreement.

ety: milā(nā) + āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. = H ملاوا मिलावा milāwā [milā(nā) = āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं], s.m. = H ملاوٿ मिलावट milāwaṭ [milā(nā)+āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं], s.f. Mixing, mingling, mixture; admixture; amalgamation; coalition; composition; annexation; adulteration; alloy; — union, concord, unison, accord; comparison; assimilation; reconciliation; adjustment; arbitration
more: n. m. 1. Mixture; admixture; commixture; composition. 2. Amalgamation; annexation. 3. Adulteration; alloy. 4. Accord; union. 5. Adjustment; reconciliation. milāo k. , v. a. 1. To mix; intermix. 2. To alloy; adulterate.
subs: (-meṅ), To mix, mingle, intermingle; to alloy, to adulterate; to effect union or reconciliation (between), &c.

def: s. m. Mixture, mixing, accord, unison, assimilation.
s.f. = milānī, and milāʼo, qq.v. s.f. Mixing, mingling; adjustment; comparison, &c. (see milāʼo).

ety: prob. i.q. milāw or milāʼo, q.v.
def: s.m. (in Hindī) and f. (in Urdū), Mixing, mingling (with); meeting, interview, visit; social converse, society; — junction; coalescence; combination; union, agreement, harmony, concord, mutual good understanding, adjustment of differences; reconciliation; pacification, peace; — agreeing, suiting, consisting well together
more: n. f. S. मिलन See اتفاق (1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7). 1. Concord. 2. Adjustment of differences; reconciliation. milāp-jog , ( Bot .) Perianthium. milāp rakhnā , v. n. To agree with; live in concord. milāp k. , v. a. To be reconciled to. milāp karvānā , v. a. To reconcile; conciliate. milāp'ī , adj. Social; friendly.
s. f. (dakh. s. m.) Agreement, reconciliation, accommodation, mixing, coalescence, combination, union, concord, understanding, adjustment, pacification, visit, meeting.
subs: (-se), to agree or harmonize (with); to live in harmony or concord (with)
(-se), To harmonize; to make friends (with), to be reconciled (to)
(-meṅ), To bring about agreement or harmony (between); to effect a reconciliation (between), to reconcile, to make peace (between).

ety: milāp, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Sociable, friendly, intimate, affable, civil; convivial; — associate, companion; a social person, &c.

ety: mil(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (āpi, caus. augment+suff. ti)
def: s.f. Society, company; friendship

r: milaunī, milonī, milāwnī, milāʼonī, milāʼonā, milānā
ety: for milāwnī or milāʼonī, fem. of milāʼonā = milānā, q.v.
def: s.f. Water or other liquor added to make a mixture; — the mixture so made; — mixture, admixture; adulteration, alloy.

ety: milā(nā) = āwā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. = H ملاو मिलाव milāʼo [milā(nā) + āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं], s.m. = H ملاوٿ मिलावट milāwaṭ [milā(nā)+āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं], s.f. Mixing, mingling, mixture; admixture; amalgamation; coalition; composition; annexation; adulteration; alloy; — union, concord, unison, accord; comparison; assimilation; reconciliation; adjustment; arbitration

def: v.n. Bālā-pan meṅ biyāh bhayo tab jhūlat rahī palnī palnā, Kar-ke biyāh ḍigar gayo balmā, chhoto ho mero jubnā, Sajnī! mohe āj mile balmā . Farrukh. Song. When I was wed a child I in the cradle swung, My spouse did leave me then, my budding breasts yet young, Today, my dear, my absent husband hath up sprung! 3. Ek kanak aur kāmnī, yeh lambī tarvār, Jāt rahe Harī milan ko līe bich hī mār . Doha. Gold and woman sharp as swords Slay those going Hari-wards. 4. Siyām baran nit sohanī ajab anokhī nār, Do se, das se, bīs se, mile woh ekhī bār . Riddle. Ans. Kanghī . A…

ety: the twenty-eighth of the Persian alphabet, and twenty-fourth of the Arabic
def: s.m. The letter, or the sound ma.
more: the 24th of the Arabic alphabet and 28th of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to लिये in Sanskrit. In abjad , q. v. it stands for 40. Under the Mugal Government, a countersign by the Dīvān upon an imperial grant or assignment of revenue. It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with b, q v. meṛhā=bheṛā a ram; minmin= bhinbhin buzz: with p , as māoṅ=pāoṅ foot; mathnā=pheṅṭnā beat up; mīsnā=pīsnā crush; chimaṭnā=chipaknā, chipaṭnā stick to; ṭīm-ṭām= ṭīp-tāp display: with h , as samet=sahit with; H. siyām = P. siyāh black: with g , as mūṅchh=goṅchh moustache: with n , as Eng. male = H. nar man; māp =nāp measure; sāmlā=sāṅolā nut-brown; nām= nāoṅ name; ṭhām=ṭhāoṅ place; S. kim = H. kin who tum=toṅ you: with l , as mathnā=lathnā to beat (an egg); māṭ=loṭ an earthen pot; simāī=silāī sewing; chamak=chilak; jhamak=jhalak lustre. As an initial and final with ṭ , as ṭasaknā=masaknā m…

the twenty-fourth letter of the Arabic, and twenty-eighth of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to म in Sanskrit. It is one of the labial letters, its sound being that of the English m. When used to denote a number, according to the numerical arrangement of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for forty. It is compared by poets to the mouth; whence it has obtained the name of ميم مرادي mīm-i-murādī: and, by way of abbreviation, it is used to signify the month muḥarram. It is sometimes changed into ن; as, بان for بام A roof: and, at others, it is substituted for ن as, کجيم for کجين A quilted tunick worn in battle. In Persian, affixed to verbs and names of qualities, it may be, 1. (ميم متکلم mīm-i-mutakallim ) denoting the pronoun of the first person singular; in which case, م is always quiescent, except for some particular reason. This admits of four subdivisions. (1.) An admiral. 2. A collector of port duties. mīr-bahrī , n. f. Port duties. mīr-bakhshī , n. m. Paymaster-general. mīr-tuzak , n. m. Chief marshal; an officer who maintains order in a march or procession. mīr jī , A title of mirāsīs , q. v. mīr sāmān , n. m. A head steward. mīr-shikār , n. m. Chief huntsman. mīr-farsh , Weights to keep down the ends of a carpet. mīr adl , n. m. Chief justice ( Mah .). A superintendent of the courts who revised the decisions of the Qāzīs and judges, and passed sentence. mīr-majlis , n. m. 1. The master of the ceremonies. 2. more: s. m. A portion paid to the head of a department out of the fees received by the inferiour officers.

prov. 'Mīrān's grave (is) as (or as large as) Mīrān'; expenditure as the income.

def: n. m. A Mah. saint much revered by Hindūs. 1. Mīrāṅ kī kaṛhāī karo! Make an offering of fried cakes to Mirān! 2. Mīrāṅ gor barābar . Prov. Mīrān's grave as Mīrān (as large). (Expenditure as the income).

(from ورث Heb. ) s. f. Heritage, patrimony, hereditary estate, a bequest ( Harris ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian میراث (mīrās), from Arabic مِيرَاث (mīrāṯ).

adj. 1. Hereditary, inherited. 2. A singer (of a cast who follow that profession from generation to generation).

more: Bhoj. n. f. S. मरिच Chilli; pepper; Capsicum frutescens . 1. Ādī mirchāī kā kaun sāth? Prov. Chillies dont go with ginger. 2. Mirchoṅ kī dhūnī do āp bhāg jāëgā . (Said of an evil spirit). mirch , adj. Hot; sharp. dakhnī mirch . White pepper. kālī mirch . Pepper. lāl mirch , n. f. Red chillis or pepper. harī mirch . Green chilli. mircheṅ lagnā , v. n. 1. To smart; feel a smart. Abhī kuckh kahūṅgā to mircheṅ sī lag jāëṅgī . 2. To take ill; take amiss.
s. f. Pepper. گول مرچ gol-mirch, Black pepper. لال مرچ lāl-mirch, Capsicum or red pepper.
subs: 'To feel as if chillies were applied,' to smart, to tingle, to burn; — to take ill or amiss
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit मरीच (marīca), from मरिच (marica).

When did I ever say that your son has epileptic fits? (She purposely makes known what she denies having said). mirgī-vālā, mirgihā, mirgiyā , n. m. One afflicted with epilepsy.
subs: (-ko), To be subject to epilepsy, to have epileptic fits

Three small delicate golden chains, worn as the ṭīkā , fixed to the hair by small hooks, the lower ends being sometimes set with precious stones. mirzā-phoyā , n. m. A very delicate person. mirzā-mizāj , adj. Fastidious; nice. mirzā'ī , n. f. Princedom. mirza'ī ; Rus. mirjaī , n. f. A native quilted coat.

(from امير Chief, and part. pass. of زادن To be born) s. m. 1. A prince. 2. A title of Musalmāns. (Plur. مرزايان). مرزابي پروا mirzā be parwā (dakh.) Three small delicate golden chains, worn as the ṭīkā, fixed to the hair by small hooks, the lower hanging ends being either set or not with precious stones: Qanoon-e-islam, App. p. xviii. مرزا مزاج mirzā-mizāj, adj. Fastidious, nice.
subs: s.m. A reckless fellow; — three small delicate golden chains, worn as the ṭīkā, fixed to the hair by small hooks (the lower ends being sometimes set with precious stones)
adj. Of a princely nature or disposition; genteel.

s. m. Pretence, sham, stratagem. ¶ masi ( मसि ) s. f. Ink.

ety: v.n. fr. مثل 'to compare, liken,' &c.; see mis̤l
def: s.f. Likeness, similitude; simile; analogy; parable, metaphor; specimen, example, model; a case adduced as a precedent; — adj. Like, resembling
more: n. f. مثل being like. 1. Likeness; similitude; simile; analogy. 2. A specimen. 3. Example ( udāharan , 1). 4. A case adduced as a precedent. misāl denā , To give an example or illustration.
(from مثل Heb. and Chald. ) s. f. Simile, likeness, similitude; parable.
subs: To give an example or illustration
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مِثَال (misāl), borrowed from Arabic مِثَال (miṯāl).

ety: rel. n. fr. mis̤āl
def: adj. Like, resembling.

ety: S. मिश्रणं
def: s.m. A soil consisting of a mixture of clay and sand.

ety: inf. n. of ماسس 'to be in contact with, to touch,' iii of مسّ 'to touch'
def: s.m. Touching; touch; mutual contact; pressing; lying with; coitus.
more: n. m. 1. Pressing; squeezing; billing and cooing. 2. Coitus .
(inf. III. of مسّ) s. m. 1. Mutual contact. 2. Coitus.

ety: corr. of Eng. miss+bābā, q.v.
def: s.f. An unmarried girl or lady (of European parentage).

ety: n. of instr. fr. صبُح 'to be or become bright'
def: s.f. A light, lamp, lantern (cf. sirāj).
more: (noun of instrument of صبح) s. f. A lamp.

ety: n. of instr. fr. صدق 'to prove true or correct'
def: s.f. Whatever confirms or proves the truth (of a thing); a verifier.
more: (from صدق) s. f. Whatever verifies or shews to be true.

def: s.m. See s.v. mis. s.m. Copper (syn. tāṅbā)
more: s. m. A brasier, a coppersmith.
subs: s.m. A worker in copper, a coppersmith; a brasier.

ety: S. मिषं
def: s.m. Rivalry, emulation; envy; — deception, deceit, fraud, trick, stratagem; sham, pretence; excuse, apology; — illusion, false appearance (i.q. mis, q.v.); — adv. Under the pretence or pretext (of, -ke); having assumed the form (of).

ety: for A. مشربة mishrabat, n. of instr. fr. شرب 'to drink'
def: s.m. A drinking-vessel (syn. piyāla).

ety: rt. मिष्
def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Sprinkled, moistened; — sweet, sugary; pleasant, agreeable; nice, savoury, dainty, delicate; — s.m. A sweetmeat; — a dainty or savoury dish
subs: (˚ṭa+an˚), s.m. Sweetmeat; sweet or savoury food, a savoury dish; frumenty; a mixture of sugar and acids eaten with rice or bread; sauce, gravy, seasoning.

def: n. m. S. मिष्टान्न Sweets. Mevah mishṭān .
s.m. See s.v. mishṭ.
adj. f. Made of vetches (māsh).

ety: mis, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Made of copper; — s.f. = missī, q.v. s.f. A powder (composed of yellow myrobalan, gall-nut, iron-filings, vitriol, &c.) used for tinging the teeth of a black colour
more: 1. s. f. A powder (made of vitriol, &c.) with which the teeth are tinged of a black colour. مسي دان misī-dān, A box of gold, silver or other metal for holding misī. 2. (from Sans. माष ) adj. f. Made of vetches.
subs: s.m. A box (of gold, silver, or other metal) for holding missī
To apply missī to the teeth and kājal or 'lamp-black' to the eyes, to beautify oneself
or missī-lagānā, or missī malnā (-meṅ), To mark lines with missī (on the teeth); to apply, or to rub, missi (to, or on, the teeth).

def: s.f. corr. of ميشی mīshī, q.v.
s.f. A sort of fennel, Anethum sowa, and A. panmori; — common anise, Pimpinella anisum; — spikenard, Nardostachys jatamansi (also written miśi; miśī; mishi).
s.f. = misi, or misī, q.v. adj. Made of copper; — s.f. = missī, q.v. s.f. A powder (composed of yellow myrobalan, gall-nut, iron-filings, vitriol, &c.) used for tinging the teeth of a black colour
subs: s.m. A box (of gold, silver, or other metal) for holding missī
To apply missī to the teeth and kājal or 'lamp-black' to the eyes, to beautify oneself
or missī-lagānā, or missī malnā (-meṅ), To mark lines with missī (on the teeth); to apply, or to rub, missi (to, or on, the teeth).

ety: v.n. fr. ثقل 'to be heavy or weighty'
def: s.m. A weight of 1 3⁄7 drachms, or of 4 māshās and 3½ rattīs (q.v.s.v. tolā).
more: (from ثقل Heb. ) s. m. A weight containing 4 māshas and 3 1/2 rattīs (which see under تولا tolā ).

def: (n. of instr. fr. صقل 'to polish'), s.m.(?) A polishing-instrument (com. a bead-shaped stone, or a smooth shell).

ety: for A. مصقلة miṣqalat
def: s.m. = miṣqal, q.v. s.m. A weight of 1 3⁄7 drachms, or of 4 māshās and 3½ rattīs (q.v.s.v. tolā).
more: (from صقل) s. An instrument with which a thing is polished.

ety: for A. مصقلة miṣqalat
def: s.m. = miṣqal, q.v. s.m. A weight of 1 3⁄7 drachms, or of 4 māshās and 3½ rattīs (q.v.s.v. tolā).
more: (from صقل) s. An instrument with which a thing is polished.

ety: v.n. fr. sukūn; see maskanat
def: adj. & s.m. Lowly, humble, submissive; meek, mild; — poor, needy, indigent, wretched, miserable; — a poor man or woman, a pauper, a miserable wretch.
more: adj. سکن was quiet, humble. 1. Meek; mild (اُدهين 2, 3). miskīn'ī , n. f. Meekness; mildness (اَدهينتا 1, 2).
(from سکن) adj. Poor, miserable, humble, wretched, indigent. (Plur. مساکين masākīn ).

ety: fr. miskīn
def: s.f. Lowliness, humility, submissiveness; meekness, mildness; — poverty, indigence, misery (syn. maskanat).
